name: overview_dashboard_phase1
outcome_goal: Phase 1 of the Overview Dashboard rebuild: delete fictional blocks (contract preview, terminal, hidden sidebar scaffolding), surface BUG-0AEFD1A1 file-inventory failure visibly, make Tool Opportunities clickable. Update Dashboard.test.tsx to assert these invariants, run vitest.

jobs.label: phase1_dashboard_source_edit
jobs.description: Delete fictional blocks from Dashboard.tsx, surface /api/files failure as visible degraded state, make Tool Opportunities rows clickable.
jobs.outcome_goal: Dashboard.tsx no longer contains the hardcoded contract preview, fake terminal block, or hidden sidebar scaffolding. The /api/files?scope=instance fetch surfaces failure as a visible 'Inventory unavailable' state in the Knowledge Base panel. Every Tool Opportunity row is a clickable button (onClick → onChat).
jobs.prompt:
You are editing the Praxis Overview Dashboard component to enforce its filed authority contract (decision_key: architecture_policy.surfaces_app_overview.dashboard_contract). File to edit: Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/dashboard/Dashboard.tsx Make exactly these changes: 1. DELETE the hardcoded contract preview block. - The JSX block `
…
` (currently around lines 818–882) is hardcoded fiction. Remove it AND the wrapping `
…
` since that wrapper exists only to host the fiction. 2. DELETE the fake terminal block. - The JSX block `
…
` (currently around lines 888–907) outputs static fake CLI text. Remove it. - If the surrounding `
` becomes a single-child wrapper after this removal, simplify but keep the receipts cards intact. 3. DELETE hidden sidebar scaffolding. - Remove the `
…
` block (CSS already hides it via `display: none`). - Remove the `
…
` block (CSS already hides it). - Remove the `
…
` element (CSS already hides it). - Do NOT touch the visible parts of the sidebar (overview spine, overview-grid, actions, bottom row). 4. SURFACE the file-inventory failure (BUG-0AEFD1A1 visibility fix). - The `useEffect` fetching `/api/files?scope=instance` (around lines 435–449) currently has a silent catch ("Silent by design…"). Replace with visible degraded state. - Add a `filesStatus` state: `'loading' | 'ok' | 'error'`. Initialize to `'loading'`, set to `'ok'` on a successful 2xx response, `'error'` on non-2xx or thrown error (still ignore AbortError). - In the Knowledge Base panel (find the `

Knowledge Base

` heading), the rendering becomes: - if `filesStatus === 'error'`: render a `dash-empty dash-empty--compact` block with title `Inventory unavailable` and copy `The /api/files?scope=instance endpoint did not respond. Upload still works, but the existing file list could not be loaded.` - if `filesStatus === 'loading'` AND `instanceFiles.length === 0`: show a `dash-empty dash-empty--compact` with title `Loading inventory…` and copy `Reading attached files from the instance scope.` - if `filesStatus === 'ok'` AND `instanceFiles.length === 0`: keep the existing `No files attached` empty state - otherwise: render the file list (existing behavior) - Keep the `Add file` action visible regardless of state. 5. TOOL OPPORTUNITIES: every row clickable. - Each rendered opportunity in the `dash-review-list` under Tool Opportunities (around lines 1126–1162) currently uses `dash-review-item--static`. Replace each with a `
jobs.verify_command: cd /Users/nate/Praxis && grep -L 'dash-contract-preview\|dash-terminal\|dash-sidebar__brand\|dash-sidebar__stack\|dash-sidebar__overview-copy\|Silent by design' 'Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/dashboard/Dashboard.tsx' >/dev/null && grep -q 'Inventory unavailable' 'Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/dashboard/Dashboard.tsx' && grep -q 'filesStatus' 'Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/dashboard/Dashboard.tsx'

jobs.label: phase1_dashboard_test_update
jobs.description: Update Dashboard.test.tsx to lock in the contract enforced by the source edit.
jobs.outcome_goal: Dashboard.test.tsx no longer asserts deleted markup. New tests cover: file-inventory failure surfaces visibly, every Tool Opportunity row is clickable, no fictional contract/terminal copy in DOM, four-tone color law (healthy/warning/danger/neutral pill classes).
jobs.prompt:
Update Dashboard.test.tsx so it locks in the contract enforced by the prior phase1_dashboard_source_edit job.

File to edit: Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/dashboard/Dashboard.test.tsx

Do all of the following:

1. Drop any expectations that depend on the deleted markup. Remove or modify any assertion on text matching `workflow_contract`, `tec_workflow_lane`, `materialized`, `sandbox · overview`, `praxis workflow query overview`, `dash-terminal`, `dash-contract-preview`, or any string from the deleted contract-preview / terminal / hidden-sidebar blocks.

2. Add a new test: `surfaces /api/files failure as visible degraded state, not silent zero`.
   - Mock fetch so `/api/files?scope=instance` returns 400 (or rejects with non-Abort error).
   - Mock `/api/dashboard` to return a valid snapshot.
   - Render `<Dashboard …callbacks />`.
   - Assert text `Inventory unavailable` is in the document.
   - Assert text `No files attached` is NOT in the document.

3. Add a new test: `every Tool Opportunity row is clickable`.
   - Mock `/api/dashboard` to include at least two `tool_opportunities` items.
   - Render the dashboard.
   - Query all rendered review items inside the Tool Opportunities panel and assert each is a `<button>` element with a defined `onClick`.

4. Add a new test: `does not render fictional contract or terminal copy`.
   - Render with any valid snapshot.
   - Assert that none of `workflow_contract`, `tec_workflow_lane`, `praxis workflow query overview`, `dash-terminal`, `dash-contract-preview` appear in the DOM.

5. Add a new test: `tone classes obey four-tone color law`.
   - Render with `summary.health.tone = 'healthy'` and assert `dash-pill--healthy` is present.
   - Re-render with `tone: 'warning'` and assert `dash-pill--warning`.
   - Re-render with `tone: 'danger'` and assert `dash-pill--danger`.
   - Re-render with `tone: 'neutral'` and assert `dash-pill--neutral`.
   - These four tones are the only canonical ones; the test fails if a fifth class is introduced.

Keep existing tests that still pass. Use the existing `vi.fn()` fetch mock pattern in this file. Do not add new dependencies.
jobs.verify_command: cd /Users/nate/Praxis/Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app && npx vitest run src/dashboard/Dashboard.test.tsx --reporter=verbose --run 2>&1 | tail -50
